keywords.label: GoToURL
keywords.helpLabel: Navigates the browser to a URL

tooltip.what: Opens a URL in the browser. Use this as the first step to land on your application.
tooltip.example:
Value: https://myapp.qa.com/login
or use Common Data: ${appUrl}
tooltip.tip: Always follow this with a Wait or Assert step to confirm the page loaded correctly.

keywords.label: Reload Page
keywords.helpLabel: Refreshes the current browser page

tooltip.what: Reloads the current page — same as pressing F5. Use after actions that require a page refresh to reflect changes.
tooltip.example: No locator or value needed. Just add the step.
tooltip.tip: Follow with Wait Page Load to ensure the page fully reloads before next step.

keywords.label: Go Back
keywords.helpLabel: Clicks the browser Back button

tooltip.what: Navigates to the previous page in browser history — same as clicking the Back button.
tooltip.example: No locator or value needed.
tooltip.tip: Use after completing an action on a detail page when you need to return to the list page.

keywords.label: Go Forward
keywords.helpLabel: Clicks the browser Forward button

tooltip.what: Navigates forward in browser history — same as clicking the Forward button.
tooltip.example: No locator or value needed.
tooltip.tip: Only works if you have previously gone Back. Rarely used in automation.

keywords.label: Click
keywords.helpLabel: Clicks a button, link or any clickable element

tooltip.what: Simulates a mouse click on a button, link, menu item, icon or any element.
tooltip.example:
Locator: button:has-text("Save")
Locator: #btn-submit
Locator: .nav-item[data-tab="reports"]
tooltip.tip: For dropdown menus: first Click the trigger to open it, then Click the option. For elements inside a table row, scope your locator: tr:has-text("RecordName") .btn-delete

keywords.label: Double Click
keywords.helpLabel: Double-clicks an element (e.g. to open edit mode)

tooltip.what: Performs two rapid clicks on an element. Some applications open edit mode or a detail view on double-click.
tooltip.example:
Locator: tr:has-text("Gateway-01")
Locator: .editable-cell
tooltip.tip: If single click does not open the editor, try Double Click instead.

keywords.label: Fill
keywords.helpLabel: Clears and types text into an input field

tooltip.what: Clears any existing content and types the given value into a text input, email, search or textarea field.
tooltip.example:
Locator: input[name="Username"]  Value: harmeet
Locator: #search-box  Value: Gateway Type
Locator: textarea#notes  Value: Test completed
tooltip.tip: ⚠️ If the value contains # or special characters (e.g. passwords like Admin#123), use Type (slow) instead — Fill may truncate at # character.

keywords.label: Type (slow)
keywords.helpLabel: Types character-by-character — safe for passwords with special characters

tooltip.what: Types the value one character at a time with a small delay between each keystroke. This is required for password fields containing # or other special characters that Fill may break.
tooltip.example:
Locator: input[name="Password"]  Value: Admin#1234
Locator: #api-key  Value: sk-abc$xyz!
tooltip.tip: Always use this for passwords. Use Fill for regular text fields.

keywords.label: Clear Field
keywords.helpLabel: Clears all text from an input field

tooltip.what: Removes all existing content from a text input. Use before Fill if the field already has a value you want to replace.
tooltip.example:
Locator: #search-input
Locator: input[name="GatewayName"]
tooltip.tip: Fill already clears before typing — you only need Clear as a separate step when clearing without typing a new value.

keywords.label: Date Picker
keywords.helpLabel: Opens a Bootstrap date picker and selects the given date

tooltip.what: Clicks the date input field to open a Bootstrap Datepicker widget, navigates to the correct month/year using the < > arrows, and clicks the target day. Handles navigation across any number of months automatically.
tooltip.example:
Locator: .bootstrapDatepicker  Value: 02 Apr 2026
Locator: #createdOn          Value: 2026-04-02
Locator: input[name=fromDate] Value: 04/02/2026
tooltip.tip: Locator should point to the date input field — clicking it opens the calendar. The calendar icon next to it is decorative. Supported formats: DD Mon YYYY · YYYY-MM-DD · MM/DD/YYYY · Mon DD YYYY.

keywords.label: Select
keywords.helpLabel: Selects an option from a <select> dropdown

tooltip.what: Chooses an option from a standard HTML dropdown (select element). Use the visible option text or the option's value attribute.
tooltip.example:
Locator: select[name="GatewayType"]  Value: HTTP
Locator: #role-dropdown  Value: Admin
tooltip.tip: ⚠️ This only works on standard <select> elements. For custom styled dropdowns (div/ul based), use Click on the trigger then Click on the option.

keywords.label: Check
keywords.helpLabel: Ticks a checkbox to the checked state

tooltip.what: Checks a checkbox if it is not already checked. Has no effect if the checkbox is already ticked.
tooltip.example:
Locator: input[name="IsActive"]
Locator: label[for="chk-enable"]
tooltip.tip: ⚠️ Many applications use styled checkboxes where the visible element is a <label>, not the <input>. If Check fails, try targeting the label: label[for="checkboxId"]

keywords.label: Uncheck
keywords.helpLabel: Unticks a checkbox to the unchecked state

tooltip.what: Unchecks a checkbox if it is currently checked. Has no effect if already unticked.
tooltip.example:
Locator: input[name="SendNotification"]
Locator: label[for="chk-notify"]
tooltip.tip: Same locator rules as Check — target the label if the native input is hidden.

keywords.label: Hover
keywords.helpLabel: Moves the mouse over an element to reveal hover menus or tooltips

tooltip.what: Moves the mouse pointer over an element without clicking. Use to trigger dropdown menus, reveal hidden buttons, or show tooltips.
tooltip.example:
Locator: .nav-menu-item:has-text("Mediation Config")
Locator: #user-avatar
tooltip.tip: Follow Hover with a Wait Visible step for the element that should appear after hovering, then Click it.

keywords.label: Focus
keywords.helpLabel: Moves keyboard focus to an element

tooltip.what: Sets the keyboard focus on an element — equivalent to clicking into a field without typing. Useful to trigger validation or reveal dynamic fields.
tooltip.example:
Locator: #email-input
Locator: input[name="SearchTerm"]
tooltip.tip: Rarely needed. Use Fill or Click for most interactions. Use Focus when an action needs to trigger just by the element receiving focus.

keywords.label: Press Key
keywords.helpLabel: Presses a keyboard key

tooltip.what: Simulates pressing a keyboard key. Common uses: submit a form with Enter, dismiss a modal with Escape, move between fields with Tab.
tooltip.example:
Value: Enter  →  submits focused form
Value: Escape  →  closes a modal or dropdown
Value: Tab  →  moves focus to next field
Value: ArrowDown  →  navigates a list
tooltip.tip: Use after Fill to submit a search form. Key names are case-sensitive: use Enter not enter.

keywords.label: Drag & Drop
keywords.helpLabel: Drags an element and drops it onto a target element

tooltip.what: Drags the source element (Locator) and drops it onto the target element (Value). Used for reordering lists, kanban boards or file drop zones.
tooltip.example:
Locator: #draggable-item-1  Value: #droptarget-zone
Locator: .kanban-card:first-child  Value: .kanban-column.done
tooltip.tip: ⚠️ Some drag-and-drop implementations require JavaScript events. If this keyword fails, the application may need a custom script.

keywords.label: Upload File
keywords.helpLabel: Uploads a file using a file input element

tooltip.what: Attaches a file to a file upload input. The locator targets the <input type="file"> element and the value is the full path to the file on disk.
tooltip.example:
Locator: input[type="file"]  Value: C:\TestData\invoice.pdf
Locator: #file-upload  Value: E:\reports\export.xlsx
tooltip.tip: The file must exist on the machine running the test. Use absolute paths.

keywords.label: Scroll To
keywords.helpLabel: Scrolls the page to bring an element into view

tooltip.what: Scrolls the page until the target element is visible in the viewport. Use before interacting with elements that are off-screen.
tooltip.example:
Locator: #save-button
Locator: .footer-section
tooltip.tip: Most interaction keywords (Click, Fill) scroll automatically. Only use this step when you need to confirm an element is in view before taking a screenshot.

keywords.label: Select All Text
keywords.helpLabel: Selects all text inside an input or text area

tooltip.what: Triple-clicks the element to select all its text content. Use before typing to replace the full content of a field.
tooltip.example:
Locator: #gateway-name-input
Locator: textarea#description
tooltip.tip: Follow with Fill or Type to replace the selected text with a new value.

keywords.label: Right Click
keywords.helpLabel: Right-clicks an element to open its context menu

tooltip.what: Performs a right-click (secondary click) on the target element. Used to open context menus, custom right-click panels, or inline action menus.
tooltip.example:
Locator: #file-row-1  →  right-click to open context menu
Locator: .grid-row:first-child  →  right-click for row actions
tooltip.tip: Follow with a Click step on the context menu option you want to select.

keywords.label: JS Click
keywords.helpLabel: Clicks an element using JavaScript — bypasses visibility checks

tooltip.what: Fires a click event via JavaScript directly on the element. Use when normal Click fails due to the element being overlaid, obscured, or not fully visible to Playwright.
tooltip.example:
Locator: #hidden-submit-btn  →  JS click a button behind an overlay
Locator: .dropdown-item[data-val='X']  →  JS click a non-interactive element
tooltip.tip: Prefer normal Click when possible. Use JS Click only for elements that Playwright cannot reach through normal interaction.

keywords.label: Select By Index
keywords.helpLabel: Selects a dropdown option by its position (0 = first)

tooltip.what: Selects a <select> option by its 0-based index position rather than by text or value. Use when option text is dynamic or unknown at script-write time.
tooltip.example:
Locator: #country-select  Value: 0  →  select first country
Locator: #priority-ddl   Value: 2  →  select third option
tooltip.tip: Index 0 = first option. Combined with Test Data rows you can select different options across test runs.

keywords.label: Drag By Offset
keywords.helpLabel: Drags an element by a pixel offset (x, y) from its current position

tooltip.what: Picks up the target element and moves it by the specified pixel offset (deltaX, deltaY). Use for sliders, resizable panels, or canvas elements where there is no fixed drop target.
tooltip.example:
Locator: #slider-handle  Value: 150,0   →  drag right 150px
Locator: .resize-handle  Value: 0,80    →  drag down 80px
tooltip.tip: Use positive X to drag right, negative X for left. Positive Y drags down, negative Y drags up.

keywords.label: Click N Times
keywords.helpLabel: Clicks an element a specified number of times

tooltip.what: Clicks the target element N times in sequence with a short delay between each click. Useful for increment/decrement buttons, quantity controls, or rating stars.
tooltip.example:
Locator: #qty-increment  Value: 3  →  click + button 3 times
Locator: .star-rating-btn Value: 4  →  click to give 4 stars
tooltip.tip: Each click includes a short 200ms wait. For rapid multi-click (triple-click to select text) use Select All instead.

keywords.label: Hover & Click
keywords.valueHint: Locator of element to click after hover
keywords.helpLabel: Hovers over an element then clicks a revealed child element

tooltip.what: Hovers over the Locator element to reveal hidden children (e.g. a dropdown, tooltip, or action menu), then clicks the element specified in the Value field.
tooltip.example:
Locator: .table-row:first-child  Value: .row-action-delete  →  hover row, click Delete
Locator: #user-avatar            Value: #menu-logout         →  hover avatar, click Logout
tooltip.tip: The Value must be a CSS selector for the element that appears after the hover. It does not use the Locator Repository — type it directly.

keywords.label: Prompt Type
keywords.valueHint: Text to type into the prompt, then accept
keywords.helpLabel: Types text into a browser prompt dialog and accepts it

tooltip.what: Intercepts a browser window.prompt() dialog, types the given text into it, and accepts it. Used when an action triggers a native browser prompt asking for user input.
tooltip.example:
Value: P-20045  →  type patient ID into a prompt dialog
Value: ConfirmDelete  →  type confirmation word into a prompt
tooltip.tip: Place this step BEFORE the action that triggers the prompt — Playwright must register the handler before the dialog opens.

keywords.label: Switch to Window
keywords.valueHint: Window index (0 = first/original) or title substring
keywords.helpLabel: Switches focus to another open browser window or tab

tooltip.what: Switches the active page context to another browser window or tab. Use index 0 for the original window, 1 for the second, etc. Or provide a partial title to match by title.
tooltip.example:
Value: 1         →  switch to the second opened window
Value: Patient Portal  →  switch to window whose title contains 'Patient Portal'
tooltip.tip: Always switch back to the main window (Value: 0) when done. Use New Tab Wait before this step if waiting for a window to open.

keywords.label: Assert Visible
keywords.helpLabel: Verifies an element is visible on the page

tooltip.what: Confirms the element exists and is visible to the user. The test fails if the element is hidden or not in the DOM.
tooltip.example:
Locator: .success-toast  →  confirm save confirmation appears
Locator: #dashboard-panel  →  confirm user is logged in
tooltip.tip: Use after Save/Submit/Login to verify the expected result appeared on screen.

keywords.label: Assert Hidden
keywords.helpLabel: Verifies an element is NOT visible (hidden or removed)

tooltip.what: Confirms the element is no longer visible — either hidden by CSS or removed from the DOM. Use after Delete to confirm a record is gone.
tooltip.example:
Locator: tr:has-text("GW-Test-01")  →  confirm record was deleted
Locator: .loading-spinner  →  confirm loading is done
tooltip.tip: Pair this with Wait Hidden if the element disappears after an animation or page refresh.

keywords.label: Assert Text
keywords.helpLabel: Verifies an element contains the expected text

tooltip.what: Checks that the element's visible text contains the expected value. The match is partial — the element just needs to include the text, not match it exactly.
tooltip.example:
Locator: .toast-message  Value: saved successfully
Locator: h1.page-title  Value: Gateway Type Configuration
Locator: #user-name  Value: harmeet
tooltip.tip: Use after form submit to verify success messages. Match is case-sensitive by default.

keywords.label: Assert Value
keywords.helpLabel: Verifies an input field contains the expected value

tooltip.what: Checks the current value inside an input, select or textarea field. Use to verify a form loaded with the correct pre-filled data.
tooltip.example:
Locator: input[name="GatewayName"]  Value: HTTP-Gateway-01
Locator: #email-field  Value: harmeet@company.com
tooltip.tip: This checks the field's value attribute, not visible text. Use Assert Text for non-input elements like labels or spans.

keywords.label: Assert Attribute
keywords.helpLabel: Verifies an element has the expected HTML attribute value

tooltip.what: Checks a specific HTML attribute on the element. Format the value as attributeName=expectedValue.
tooltip.example:
Locator: #status-badge  Value: class=active
Locator: a.download-link  Value: href=/reports/export.pdf
Locator: #submit-btn  Value: disabled=true
tooltip.tip: Useful for checking CSS class states (active, disabled, selected) or link targets.

keywords.label: Assert Count
keywords.helpLabel: Verifies the number of matching elements on the page

tooltip.what: Counts all elements matching the locator and compares to the expected number. Use to verify how many rows, items or results are shown.
tooltip.example:
Locator: tbody tr  Value: 5  →  table has exactly 5 rows
Locator: .search-result-item  Value: 3  →  search returned 3 results
Locator: .error-message  Value: 0  →  no errors shown
tooltip.tip: Use Value: 0 to assert that nothing matched — equivalent to Assert Hidden but for multiple elements.

keywords.label: Assert URL
keywords.helpLabel: Verifies the current browser URL

tooltip.what: Checks that the current page URL contains the expected value. Use after navigation or login to confirm you landed on the right page.
tooltip.example:
Value: /dashboard  →  confirm redirect after login
Value: /mediation/gateway-type  →  confirm correct module opened
Value: https://myapp.com/reports
tooltip.tip: Partial match is used — the URL just needs to contain the value. You don't need to specify the full URL.

keywords.label: Assert Title
keywords.helpLabel: Verifies the browser tab page title

tooltip.what: Checks the HTML <title> of the page — the text shown in the browser tab.
tooltip.example:
Value: Gateway Type Configuration
Value: QA Agent Platform
tooltip.tip: Less commonly used. Prefer Assert URL or Assert Visible for post-navigation checks.

keywords.label: Assert Checked
keywords.helpLabel: Verifies a checkbox is in the checked (ticked) state

tooltip.what: Confirms the checkbox is currently ticked. Fails if it is unchecked.
tooltip.example:
Locator: input[name="IsActive"]
Locator: #chk-notifications
tooltip.tip: Use after Check to verify the action worked, or to verify pre-configured settings on a form.

keywords.label: Assert Enabled
keywords.helpLabel: Verifies a button or input is enabled (clickable)

tooltip.what: Confirms the element is enabled and interactive — not grayed out or disabled.
tooltip.example:
Locator: #btn-submit  →  confirm Save button is active
Locator: input[name="Email"]  →  confirm field is editable
tooltip.tip: Use to verify that required fields have been filled in before the Submit button becomes active.

keywords.label: Assert Disabled
keywords.helpLabel: Verifies a button or input is disabled (greyed out)

tooltip.what: Confirms the element is disabled and cannot be interacted with.
tooltip.example:
Locator: #btn-save  →  confirm Save is disabled until form is filled
Locator: input[name="Id"]  →  confirm ID field is read-only
tooltip.tip: Use to verify form validation behaviour — e.g. that Save stays disabled until all required fields are filled.

keywords.label: Assert Contains
keywords.helpLabel: Verifies an element's content contains given text

tooltip.what: Similar to Assert Text but specifically checks partial text containment within an element's full content.
tooltip.example:
Locator: .notification-panel  Value: updated
Locator: #activity-log  Value: harmeet logged in
tooltip.tip: Use when the element has long content and you only need to verify part of it is present.

keywords.label: Wait For Toast
keywords.helpLabel: Waits for any toast / notification message to appear

tooltip.what: Pauses the test until a toast, snackbar or alert notification appears anywhere on the page. No locator or text value needed — detects any visible notification element.
tooltip.example:
No locator or value needed.
Use after: Save, Submit, Delete, Login — any action that triggers a system message.
tooltip.tip: Use when you only need to confirm a notification appeared but don't need to check its exact text. Use Assert Toast when you need to verify the specific message.

keywords.label: Assert Toast
keywords.helpLabel: Waits for a toast message and verifies its text

tooltip.what: Waits for a toast, snackbar or alert notification to appear and checks that it contains the expected text. No locator needed — scans the full page for any notification element.
tooltip.example:
Value: saved successfully  →  after Save button
Value: deleted  →  after Delete action
Value: Login failed  →  after bad credentials
tooltip.tip: Partial match — the toast just needs to contain the value, not match exactly. Case-insensitive. Use Wait For Toast instead if the message text varies per run.

keywords.label: Assert Not Contains
keywords.valueHint: Text that must NOT appear in the element
keywords.helpLabel: Verifies an element's content does NOT contain the given text

tooltip.what: Asserts that the target element's text does not include the specified value. Use for negative checks — confirming an error message is gone, a deleted item no longer appears, etc.
tooltip.example:
Locator: #error-panel  Value: invalid  →  confirm no error message
Locator: .patient-list  Value: P-20045  →  confirm deleted patient is removed
tooltip.tip: Pair with Assert Visible first to confirm the element itself is present, then use Assert Not Contains to check its content.

keywords.label: Assert Count GreaterThan
keywords.valueHint: Minimum count (exclusive, e.g. 0 means at least 1)
keywords.helpLabel: Verifies the element count is greater than the given number

tooltip.what: Asserts that the number of matching elements exceeds the given value. Use to confirm at least N results exist — e.g. 'at least 1 search result', 'more than 0 rows in grid'.
tooltip.example:
Locator: .search-result-row  Value: 0  →  at least 1 result returned
Locator: .patient-card       Value: 4  →  more than 4 patients in list
tooltip.tip: Value 0 means 'at least 1 element exists'. This is the most common usage — confirming a list is not empty.

keywords.label: Assert Count LessThan
keywords.valueHint: Maximum count (exclusive, e.g. 10 means fewer than 10)
keywords.helpLabel: Verifies the element count is less than the given number

tooltip.what: Asserts that the number of matching elements is fewer than the given value. Use to enforce upper limits — e.g. 'no more than 5 errors shown', 'fewer than 10 items in queue'.
tooltip.example:
Locator: .error-item   Value: 5   →  fewer than 5 errors
Locator: .queue-entry  Value: 10  →  queue has fewer than 10 items
tooltip.tip: Combine with Assert Count GT to assert a range: count is between N and M.

keywords.label: Assert Greater Than
keywords.helpLabel: Verifies a numeric value in an element is greater than the threshold

tooltip.what: Reads the text of the target element, parses it as a number, and asserts it is greater than the given value. Use for price checks, score validations, quantity assertions.
tooltip.example:
Locator: #total-price  Value: 0    →  price must be positive
Locator: #score-badge  Value: 50   →  score must be above 50
Locator: #stock-count  Value: 100  →  more than 100 in stock
tooltip.tip: Non-numeric characters (£, $, commas) are stripped before comparison. Works for currency, percentages and plain numbers.

keywords.label: Assert Less Than
keywords.helpLabel: Verifies a numeric value in an element is less than the threshold

tooltip.what: Reads the text of the target element, parses it as a number, and asserts it is less than the given value. Use for validating caps, limits or maximum allowed values.
tooltip.example:
Locator: #error-count   Value: 1    →  zero errors (count < 1)
Locator: #response-time Value: 500  →  response under 500ms
Locator: #price-field   Value: 999  →  price below cap
tooltip.tip: Non-numeric characters (£, $, commas) are stripped before comparison.

keywords.label: Assert URL Not
keywords.valueHint: URL or partial URL that must NOT be current
keywords.helpLabel: Verifies the current page URL does NOT contain the given value

tooltip.what: Asserts that the browser's current URL does not contain the specified text. Use to confirm a redirect did NOT happen, or that the user is not on an error/login page.
tooltip.example:
Value: /login   →  confirm user was NOT redirected to login
Value: /error   →  confirm no error page appeared
Value: /403     →  confirm no forbidden page
tooltip.tip: Partial match — just needs to NOT appear anywhere in the URL. Use Assert URL to confirm the expected destination.

keywords.label: Assert Title Not
keywords.valueHint: Text that must NOT appear in the page title
keywords.helpLabel: Verifies the page title does NOT contain the given text

tooltip.what: Asserts that the browser tab title does not contain the specified value. Use to confirm an error page or wrong section did not load.
tooltip.example:
Value: 404       →  confirm page title is not a 404 error
Value: Forbidden →  confirm user has access
Value: Login     →  confirm user is still authenticated
tooltip.tip: Partial match against the full page title. Case-sensitive.

keywords.label: Assert Attr Not
keywords.helpLabel: Verifies an element's attribute does NOT have the given value

tooltip.what: Asserts that an element's attribute value does NOT equal the expected value. Use to confirm a state change happened — e.g. 'aria-disabled is no longer true', 'class no longer contains active'.
tooltip.example:
Locator: #save-btn   Value: disabled=true   →  button is no longer disabled
Locator: #menu-item  Value: aria-selected=false  →  item is now selected
tooltip.tip: Format: attributeName=unexpectedValue. The assertion passes if the attribute does not exist OR has a different value.

keywords.label: Assert Attr Contains
keywords.helpLabel: Verifies an element's attribute contains the given partial value

tooltip.what: Asserts that an element's attribute value contains (partial match) the expected text. Most useful for class attribute checks where a class name is one of many.
tooltip.example:
Locator: #nav-patients  Value: class=active     →  tab has the active class
Locator: #status-badge  Value: class=badge-green →  badge has green styling
Locator: #modal         Value: class=visible     →  modal is open
tooltip.tip: Format: attributeName=partialValue. For class checks, just include the class name fragment — no need for the full class string.

keywords.label: Wait For Element
keywords.helpLabel: Waits until an element appears in the DOM

tooltip.what: Pauses the test until the element exists in the page DOM. Use before interacting with elements that load dynamically.
tooltip.example:
Locator: #modal-dialog  →  wait for modal to appear
Locator: .search-results  →  wait for results to load
tooltip.tip: Always use Wait steps instead of fixed time delays. This makes tests faster and more reliable.

keywords.label: Wait Visible
keywords.helpLabel: Waits until an element becomes visible on screen

tooltip.what: Pauses until the element is visible to the user (in DOM and not hidden). More reliable than Wait For Element when elements are present but hidden.
tooltip.example:
Locator: .dropdown-menu  →  wait for dropdown options to appear
Locator: #success-banner  →  wait for confirmation message
tooltip.tip: Use this before clicking dynamically shown elements like dropdown options, modals, or toast messages.

keywords.label: Wait Hidden
keywords.helpLabel: Waits until an element disappears from view

tooltip.what: Pauses until the element is no longer visible. Use after closing modals or waiting for loading spinners to finish.
tooltip.example:
Locator: .loading-spinner  →  wait for page load to complete
Locator: #modal-dialog  →  wait for modal to close after confirm
tooltip.tip: After clicking OK on a modal, use Wait Hidden on the modal to confirm it closed before proceeding.

keywords.label: Wait PageLoad
keywords.helpLabel: Waits for the full page to finish loading

tooltip.what: Waits until the browser reports the page has fully loaded (networkidle state). Use after navigation, form submit or any action that triggers a page reload.
tooltip.example:
No locator or value needed.
Use after: GOTO, form Submit, page-level actions
tooltip.tip: Add this after every GOTO and after every form submission that causes a full page reload.

keywords.label: Wait Navigation
keywords.helpLabel: Waits for page navigation to complete after a click

tooltip.what: Waits for the browser to complete a navigation triggered by a link click or button that redirects to a new page.
tooltip.example:
No locator or value needed.
Use after clicking a navigation link that changes the URL.
tooltip.tip: Use specifically after Click steps that cause a page redirect. For ajax-only actions, use Wait Page Load instead.

keywords.label: Wait For API Response
keywords.helpLabel: Waits for a specific API/network response before continuing

tooltip.what: Pauses the test until a network response matching the URL pattern is received. Useful after form submits that call an API in the background.
tooltip.example:
Value: /api/gateways  →  wait for gateway list to reload
Value: /api/users/save  →  wait for save API to respond
tooltip.tip: Use when the page updates via AJAX without a full reload. Partial URL match is fine — /api/gateways matches any URL containing that path.

keywords.label: Wait Enabled
keywords.helpLabel: Waits until a button or input becomes enabled (not disabled)

tooltip.what: Pauses the test until the target element is no longer disabled. Extremely common after form fill — the Save/Submit button only activates once required fields are valid.
tooltip.example:
Locator: #save-btn  →  wait until Save is enabled before clicking
Locator: #submit-form  →  wait until form submit becomes active
tooltip.tip: Always use before Click on a button that starts disabled. Prevents click-on-disabled errors that silently fail without this wait.

keywords.label: Wait For Text
keywords.valueHint: Text to wait for inside the element
keywords.helpLabel: Waits until an element's text contains the expected value

tooltip.what: Pauses the test until the target element contains the specified text. Essential for async updates where element text changes after an API call completes.
tooltip.example:
Locator: #status-badge  Value: Approved  →  wait until status changes to Approved
Locator: .result-count  Value: 24 results  →  wait for search results count to update
tooltip.tip: Text match is partial and case-sensitive by default. Use after actions that trigger background processing (e.g. file processing, batch operations).

keywords.label: Wait For Alert
keywords.helpLabel: Waits for a browser alert dialog to appear

tooltip.what: Pauses the test until a native browser alert dialog appears. Use before Accept Dialog or Dismiss Dialog when the alert is triggered asynchronously (e.g. after an API response).
tooltip.example:
No locator or value needed.
Use before: Accept Dialog or Dismiss Dialog
Scenario: delete confirmation alert that appears after a server response
tooltip.tip: For immediate alerts triggered directly by a button click, Accept Dialog placed BEFORE the Click is usually sufficient. Use Wait Alert only for delayed/async alerts.

keywords.label: Wait Disabled
keywords.helpLabel: Waits until an element becomes disabled

tooltip.what: Pauses the test until the target element has the disabled attribute. Use in scenarios where an action disables a control (e.g. a Submit button disables after click to prevent double-submit).
tooltip.example:
Locator: #submit-btn  →  wait until submit is disabled (processing started)
Locator: #edit-btn    →  wait until edit button is locked during save
tooltip.tip: Useful for verifying that double-submission protection is working correctly.

keywords.label: Accept Dialog
keywords.helpLabel: Clicks OK / Accept on a browser confirm, alert or prompt dialog

tooltip.what: Handles browser-native dialogs (confirm, alert, prompt) by clicking the OK/Accept button. Place this step BEFORE the action that triggers the dialog.
tooltip.example:
Step 1: Accept Dialog (no locator/value needed)
Step 2: Click Delete Button
→ When Delete triggers 'Are you sure?' dialog, OK is clicked automatically.
tooltip.tip: Always place Accept Dialog BEFORE the step that opens the dialog, not after. Playwright registers the handler in advance.

keywords.label: Dismiss Dialog
keywords.helpLabel: Clicks Cancel / Dismiss on a browser confirm or prompt dialog

tooltip.what: Handles browser-native dialogs (confirm, prompt) by clicking the Cancel/Dismiss button. Place this step BEFORE the action that triggers the dialog.
tooltip.example:
Step 1: Dismiss Dialog (no locator/value needed)
Step 2: Click Delete Button
→ When Delete triggers 'Are you sure?' dialog, Cancel is clicked automatically.
tooltip.tip: Always place Dismiss Dialog BEFORE the step that opens the dialog. Use Accept Dialog when you want to confirm the action instead.

keywords.label: Switch to Frame
keywords.helpLabel: Switches the test context into an iframe

tooltip.what: Moves the test focus inside an embedded iframe. All subsequent steps will interact with elements inside that iframe until you Switch to Main Frame.
tooltip.example:
Locator: iframe#content-frame
Locator: iframe[name="editor"]
tooltip.tip: ⚠️ If your locators stop working after a Click, check if the page loaded content inside an iframe. Use this step to enter it first.

keywords.label: Switch to Main Frame
keywords.helpLabel: Returns focus from an iframe back to the main page

tooltip.what: Exits the current iframe and returns test focus to the main page. Always use this after finishing actions inside a Switch to Frame block.
tooltip.example: No locator or value needed.
tooltip.tip: If you forget this step after working inside an iframe, subsequent locators on the main page will fail.

keywords.label: Wait for New Tab
keywords.helpLabel: Waits for a new browser tab or popup to open

tooltip.what: Pauses and switches focus to a newly opened browser tab or popup window. Use when a Click opens a link in a new tab.
tooltip.example:
No locator or value needed.
Place this immediately after the Click that opens the new tab.
tooltip.tip: After this step, all subsequent interactions happen in the new tab. Use Close Tab to return to the original.

keywords.label: Close Tab
keywords.helpLabel: Closes the current browser tab

tooltip.what: Closes the currently active browser tab and returns focus to the previous tab.
tooltip.example: No locator or value needed.
tooltip.tip: Use after completing actions in a popup tab to return to the main application window.

keywords.label: Screenshot
keywords.helpLabel: Captures a full-page screenshot at this point in the test

tooltip.what: Takes a screenshot of the entire visible page and saves it with the test results. Use to capture evidence of test execution.
tooltip.example:
No locator or value needed.
Best placed after key actions: after login, after save, at the end of the test.
tooltip.tip: Always add a Screenshot as the last step of every test. It acts as visual proof that the test completed correctly.

keywords.label: Screenshot Element
keywords.helpLabel: Captures a screenshot of a specific element only

tooltip.what: Takes a cropped screenshot of just the specified element. Use to capture a specific widget, chart, table or form.
tooltip.example:
Locator: #results-table  →  capture just the results table
Locator: .chart-container  →  capture a specific chart
tooltip.tip: Use when you want focused evidence of a specific UI component without capturing the whole page.

keywords.label: Get Attribute
keywords.helpLabel: Reads an element attribute and saves it as a variable

tooltip.what: Reads the specified attribute from the target element and stores the result in a variable. The variable name is set in the 'Save As' field. Use {{var.name}} in any later step to reference it.
tooltip.example:
Locator: #patient-row-1  Value: data-patient-id  Save As: patientId
Locator: .download-link  Value: href            Save As: downloadUrl
Locator: #status-badge   Value: data-status     Save As: currentStatus
tooltip.tip: Works with any HTML attribute — data-*, aria-*, href, src, class, id. The value is saved as a string. If the attribute does not exist, the variable is set to empty string.

keywords.label: Get Current URL
keywords.helpLabel: Captures the current page URL and saves it as a variable

tooltip.what: Reads the browser's current URL and stores the full URL string in a variable. Set the variable name in the 'Save As' field. Useful for capturing redirected URLs or dynamic page links.
tooltip.example:
No locator or value needed.
Save As: currentUrl  →  captures e.g. https://app.example.com/patients/P-20045
Save As: redirectUrl  →  captures URL after a login redirect
tooltip.tip: Use after navigation or a redirect to capture where the app landed. Reference later with {{var.currentUrl}}.

keywords.label: Get Alert Text
keywords.helpLabel: Captures the message text from a browser alert dialog

tooltip.what: Intercepts the next browser alert dialog, reads its message text, stores it in a variable (Save As field), then dismisses the dialog. Use when you need to assert or log the exact alert message content.
tooltip.tip: Place this step BEFORE the action that triggers the alert — Playwright must register the handler before the dialog opens. The dialog is automatically dismissed after capture.

keywords.label: Get Network Response
keywords.helpLabel: Waits for an API response and saves the response body as a variable

tooltip.what: Waits for a network response whose URL contains the given pattern, reads the JSON response body, and stores it as a string in the Save As variable. Use to capture API-generated IDs, tokens or data for use in later steps.
tooltip.example:
Value: /api/patients/create  Save As: createResponse  →  captures {patientId:'P-20045',...}
Value: /api/auth/login      Save As: authResponse    →  captures {token:'abc123',...}
tooltip.tip: The response body is stored as a JSON string. Use EVALUATE in a later step with JSON.parse({{var.createResponse}}) to extract specific fields.

keywords.label: Call Function
keywords.helpLabel: Runs a reusable Common Function (e.g. login, logout, navigate to module)

tooltip.what: Executes a pre-defined Common Function — a saved sequence of steps that can be reused across many test scripts. Use this for repetitive flows like Login, Logout or standard navigation.
tooltip.example:
Function: Login as Admin  →  runs FILL username + FILL password + CLICK submit
Function: Navigate to Gateway Config  →  clicks through the menu to the target page
tooltip.tip: Define your reusable flows in the Common Functions module first, then call them here. This keeps your scripts short and maintainable.

keywords.label: Log Message
keywords.valueHint: Message to write to the test log
keywords.helpLabel: Writes a custom message to the test execution log

tooltip.what: Adds a descriptive message to the test run output. Use to mark important checkpoints in the log for easier debugging.
tooltip.example:
Value: Login completed successfully
Value: About to click Delete — verifying record exists first
Value: Test data: username = harmeet
tooltip.tip: Use Log steps at the start of major sections in your test to make the output easier to read when debugging failures.

keywords.label: File Chooser
keywords.helpLabel: Clicks a Choose File button and selects a file from the OS dialog

tooltip.what: Intercepts the native OS file picker dialog that opens when a Choose File / Browse button is clicked. Sets the selected file without the dialog ever appearing on screen. Use this when UPLOAD FILE does not work because the input is hidden behind a button.
tooltip.example:
Locator: #choose-file-btn   Value: C:\Users\test\Downloads\data.xlsx
Locator: .browse-button    Value: C:\Reports\export.csv
tooltip.tip: Locator must point to the button that opens the file dialog (not the hidden input). Value is the full path to the file on the machine running the test. After this step, click the Upload / Submit button as a separate CLICK step.

keywords.label: Set Variable
keywords.helpLabel: Captures a value from the page and saves it for use in later steps

tooltip.what: Reads a value from the page (element text, input value, or JavaScript expression) and stores it under a name. Choose 📌 Session scope (this script only) or 🌐 Global scope (shared across all scripts in the suite). Use {{var.name}} in any later step's value field to reference it.
tooltip.example:
Source: Text on page   Locator: #order-ref   Name: orderId   Scope: Session
Source: Value in field  Locator: #patient-id  Name: patientId Scope: Global
Source: JavaScript      JS: document.title    Name: pageTitle Scope: Session
tooltip.tip: Use 🌐 Global scope when a value created in Script 1 (e.g. patientId) is needed in Script 2 or later. Use 📌 Session scope when the variable is only needed within the same script. Pin icon on FILL/TYPE steps also supports both scopes.

keywords.label: Run JavaScript
keywords.helpLabel: Executes custom JavaScript directly in the browser

tooltip.what: Runs a JavaScript snippet in the browser context. Use for advanced scenarios that cannot be handled by standard keywords.
tooltip.example:
Value: document.title  →  get page title
Value: localStorage.clear()  →  clear local storage
Value: document.querySelector('#hidden-btn').click()
tooltip.tip: ⚠️ Advanced use only. Use standard keywords wherever possible. Reserve this for edge cases where no other keyword works.

keywords.label: Call API
keywords.helpLabel: Makes an HTTP API call and optionally saves the response

tooltip.what:
Sends an HTTP request (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH) to a URL and optionally saves the response body as a variable. Supports JSON body and custom headers. URL and body values support {{var.name}} tokens.

Format: METHOD url
Body (optional): JSON string in description field prefixed with BODY:
Headers: JSON string in description field prefixed with HEADERS:
tooltip.tip: Use Save As (📌 pin) to store the response body. Parse JSON fields in a later EVALUATE step: JSON.parse({{var.apptResponse}}).id. Supports {{var.x}} tokens in URL and body.

keywords.label: 📅 Date Tokens (reference)
keywords.helpLabel: Use date tokens in any value field to insert dynamic dates at run time

tooltip.what:
Date tokens are resolved at spec-generation time and replaced with real date values. Use them inside any value field — FILL, ASSERT TEXT, LOG, etc.

{{date.today}} — today's date as YYYY-MM-DD
{{date.format('DD/MM/YYYY')}} — today in custom format
{{date.add(7,'days')}} — 7 days from today
{{date.subtract(1,'month')}} — 1 month ago
{{date.diff('2026-01-01','2026-04-19','days')}} — days between two dates
tooltip.tip: Supported units: days, months, years, hours. Supported formats: DD, MM, MMM, YYYY, YY, HH, mm, ss — combine freely e.g. 'DD-MMM-YYYY' → 19-Apr-2026.

keywords.label: Assert File Downloaded
keywords.valueHint: Filename or partial filename (e.g. report.pdf or export)
keywords.helpLabel: Verifies a file was downloaded after a download action

tooltip.what: Waits for a download event triggered by the previous action and asserts the downloaded filename contains the expected text. Use after clicking a Download / Export button.
tooltip.example:
Value: patient-export.xlsx  →  verify patient export downloaded
Value: invoice            →  verify any file with 'invoice' in name downloaded
Value: .pdf               →  verify a PDF was downloaded
tooltip.tip: Place this step immediately after the CLICK step that triggers the download. Partial filename match — you don't need the full name.

keywords.label: Assert Download Count
keywords.helpLabel: Verifies the exact number of files downloaded in this test

tooltip.what: Asserts that exactly N download events occurred during this test run. Use after bulk export operations to confirm the expected number of files were generated.
tooltip.example:
Value: 1  →  exactly one file downloaded
Value: 3  →  three files downloaded in a bulk export
Value: 0  →  no downloads occurred (negative check)
tooltip.tip: Counts downloads from the start of the current test() block. Does not check the file system — checks Playwright's download event counter.

keywords.label: Read Excel Value
keywords.helpLabel: Reads a cell value from an Excel file and saves it as a variable

tooltip.what: Reads a specific cell from an Excel (.xlsx) file on disk and stores the value in a variable (Save As field). Use for data-driven tests where test inputs come from a spreadsheet.
tooltip.example:
Value: test-data/patients.xlsx|Sheet1|2|1  Save As: patientName  →  row 2, col 1
Value: data/orders.xlsx|Orders|1|4         Save As: orderTotal   →  row 1, col 4
tooltip.tip: Row and column numbers are 1-based. Sheet name is case-sensitive. The file path is relative to the project root.

keywords.label: Assert Excel Row Count
keywords.helpLabel: Verifies the number of data rows in an Excel sheet

tooltip.what: Opens an Excel file and counts the number of non-empty rows in the specified sheet. Asserts the count matches the expected value. Use to verify export completeness.
tooltip.example:
Value: exports/patient-list.xlsx|Sheet1|50  →  verify 50 patients exported
Value: reports/daily.xlsx|Data|1           →  verify at least 1 row in daily report
tooltip.tip: Header row is not counted. Only rows with at least one non-empty cell are counted. File path is relative to the project root.

keywords.label: Read PDF Text
keywords.helpLabel: Reads text from a PDF file and verifies it contains expected content

tooltip.what: Opens a PDF file from disk and searches for the expected text anywhere in the document. Use to verify downloaded invoices, reports, or letters contain the correct content.
tooltip.example:
Value: downloads/invoice-123.pdf|Total Amount: £450.00  →  verify invoice total
Value: reports/summary.pdf|Patient ID: P-20045         →  verify patient ID in report
tooltip.tip: Text search is partial and case-sensitive. The PDF must be a text-based PDF (not a scanned image). File path is relative to the project root.

tooltip.what: Intercepts a network request matching the URL pattern and returns a mocked response instead of hitting the real server. Useful for isolating tests from backend availability or testing error states.
tooltip.example:
Value: /api/patients|200|{"data":[]}  →  returns empty list for patient API
Value: /api/login|401|{"error":"Unauthorized"}  →  simulate auth failure
tooltip.tip: The URL pattern is a substring match. Set the mock BEFORE the action that triggers the request. Mock is active for the lifetime of the test.

tooltip.what: Freezes the browser's internal clock to a fixed date and time. Date pickers, countdowns, and any JS using new Date() will see this frozen time for the rest of the test.
tooltip.example:
Value: 2026-01-01T00:00:00  →  test New Year's Day logic
Value: 2026-12-31T23:59:59  →  test end-of-year edge cases
tooltip.tip: Use ISO 8601 format. The clock stays frozen until the test ends. Combine with ASSERT TEXT to verify date-dependent UI elements show the expected value.

tooltip.what: Simulates a network outage (true) or restores connectivity (false). Use to test how the app behaves when the user loses internet access — offline banners, retry logic, cached content.
tooltip.example:
Value: true  →  simulate network loss, then assert offline banner is visible
Value: false  →  restore connection, then assert data reloads
tooltip.tip: Always restore connectivity (false) before assertions that need the server. Navigation after going offline will fail — this keyword only affects subsequent requests.

tooltip.what: Asserts that an element has the specified ARIA attribute with the expected value. Use to verify accessibility markup — screen-reader labels, roles, expanded state, disabled state.
tooltip.example:
Value: aria-label=Close dialog  →  verify close button is labelled for screen readers
Value: aria-expanded=true  →  verify accordion panel is open
Value: aria-disabled=false  →  verify submit button is enabled
tooltip.tip: Format: attributeName=expectedValue. The assertion is exact-match. For partial match use ASSERT ATTR CONTAINS instead.
